title: athyrium conilii var. oldhamii
intro: Athyrium conilii var. oldhamii, commonly known as Oldham's Lady Fern, is a variety of the Lady Fern plant species (Athyrium conilii) native to China and Japan. It is a medicinal plant traditionally used to treat various health conditions, including fever, rheumatism, and skin issues, due to its antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory properties. The plant's medicinal properties are attributed to its rich content of flavonoids, alkaloids, and saponins, which have been found to have antioxidant, anti-inflammatory, and antimicrobial effects, providing health benefits such as reducing inflammation, improving immune function, and promoting wound healing. In traditional Chinese medicine, the plant is often prepared as an herbal tea, infusion, or decoction to treat various health conditions, and its herbal preparations are also used to make topical ointments and creams for skin issues.
